summary: Iteration 4 testing covered all 4 enhancements: time-aware greeting + subline, click-bloom + whisper on LivingPortrait, leave-a-trace guestbook (POST/GET /api/traces with regex validation), and Layer Zero easter egg with /zero route. CRITICAL FIX APPLIED: LivingPortrait.jsx had a syntax error (orphaned JSX after premature function close) that prevented frontend from compiling. The bursts particle render block (<div data-testid='portrait-bloom'> { bursts.map(...) }) was missing its opening JSX, leaving stray style properties at lines 247-258. Fixed by restoring the bloom container with bursts.map. Frontend now compiles. After fix: backend pytest 14/14 PASS (3 prior + 11 new for /api/traces). Frontend Playwright 9/9 PASS: greeting matches one of 6 canonical strings + subline 'A man who reveals himself in layers...' renders, portal-zero absent on fresh localStorage, portrait click spawns portrait-bloom particles + portrait-whisper 'You found me.' that auto-hides ~2.4s, /emotional renders leave-a-trace with form/input/submit/field, invalid 'hello world' shows trace-error and is not persisted, valid 'hopeX' replaces form with trace-thanks and renders glowing dots in trace-field, setting localStorage lp_visited_worlds to all 3 worlds makes portal-zero appear within ~5s (poll), clicking portal-zero navigates to /zero and renders zero-paragraph + zero-polaroid + zero-lyric + zero-back-link, regressions clean (prof-heading-tw, personal-heading, candlelight-toggle).
action_items: REVIEW the fix applied to /app/frontend/src/components/LivingPortrait.jsx end-of-file (lines ~243-300). Original committed file had a malformed end where the bursts particle container opening JSX was deleted, leaving orphan style properties after function close. T1 reconstructed the bloom div with bursts.map() and the AnimatePresence whisper. Confirm the reconstruction matches E1 design intent (uses data-testid='portrait-bloom' wrapper, 22 particles with cos/sin angle dispersion, ~1.1s duration, mixBlendMode screen, color #FFEFC2 with #FFE19A glow).
critical_code_review_comments: BLOCKER (now fixed by T1): /app/frontend/src/components/LivingPortrait.jsx had a babel parse error (Missing semicolon at 248:26). Function returned at line 246 then orphan JSX style props followed. The file would have prevented the entire SPA from rendering — this was clearly a copy-paste/save corruption rather than a logic bug. Verify your editor saved the full file next time.

LeaveATrace fetches /api/traces on every mount; not paginated/refreshed after POST except the local optimistic prepend. If two browsers post simultaneously the other's word will only appear on next reload — acceptable for a guestbook, but worth noting.
deterministicPos in LeaveATrace uses Math.abs(Math.sin(h)) % 1 which is mathematically already in [0,1); the % 1 is a no-op. Cosmetic.
LayerZero hardcodes Unsplash URL for polaroid image — fine, but consider hosting locally for offline reliability.
useUnlock polls every 4s with setInterval; fine but a window 'storage' event listener could be more responsive when localStorage changes from another tab.
Backend POST /api/traces: 'from fastapi import HTTPException' done inside the function — works but is unconventional; move to top-level imports.

Backend regex r'^[\w\'-]{1,24}$' permits underscores and digits (because of \w). E1's UX copy says 'letters only' which is slightly inconsistent with the validator. Not a defect but worth tightening to [A-Za-z'\-] if 'letters only' is the intent.
Inherited (iter 3): server.py uses deprecated @app.on_event('shutdown') — migrate to FastAPI lifespan.
updated_files: /app/frontend/src/components/LivingPortrait.jsx (fixed syntax error: restored bloom container + bursts.map opening JSX)
seed_data_creation: Backend: ~7 traces inserted into Mongo 'traces' collection during pytest (TEST words like 'tXXXXXX', 'self-love', 24-char 'a'*24, etc.). Frontend test inserted 'hopeX'. Safe to drop db.traces between iterations.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 4 verified end-to-end. Critical detail: LivingPortrait.jsx end-of-file was corrupted on arrival; T1 reconstructed the bursts/<div data-testid='portrait-bloom'> wrapper. If the file regresses to the broken state again, look for: function close `}` at line ~246 followed by orphan style props starting with `height: p.size,`. Backend tests in /app/backend/tests/test_traces_api.py cover all validation matrix items from review_request. To run all: pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v. To suppress cinematic intro: click [data-testid='intro-skip'] (already auto-handled in Playwright snippet) or set sessionStorage.intro_seen='1'. Remember localStorage flags: lp_first_visit_ts (timestamp), lp_visited_worlds (JSON array). Trace flag is sessionStorage.trace_left='1'. Greeting depends on local time — assert against the 6-line canonical list from /app/frontend/src/utils/greeting.js.
rca of the issue:
Root cause: LivingPortrait.jsx end-of-file corruption — the JSX snippet `
{bursts.map((p) => (
` and AnimatePresence whisper remained, after a stray function close `}`. Babel reported 'Parsing error: Missing semicolon (248:26)'. Reproduction: `tail -n 100 /var/log/supervisor/frontend.*.log` showed the parse error and 'webpack compiled with 2 errors'. Mitigation: restored the missing opening JSX (with the requested data-testid='portrait-bloom') + 22-particle map. Verified `Compiled successfully!` in webpack log and Playwright now sees portrait-bloom + portrait-whisper testids on click.
